essentialColumn.title: Essential Goals
essentialColumn.lead: To help healthcare organizations address common vulnerabilities by setting a floor of safeguards that will better protect them from cyber attacks, improve response when events occur, and minimize residual risk.
essentialColumn.noteItalic: To aid in further understanding the alignment to HICP we have included the links to the HICP sub-practices page for each CPG.

enhancedColumn.title: Enhanced Goals
enhancedColumn.lead: To help healthcare organizations mature their cybersecurity capabilities and reach the next level of defense needed to protect against additional attack vectors.
enhancedColumn.noteItalic: To aid in further understanding the alignment to HICP we have included the links to the HICP sub-practices page for each CPG.

purposeParagraphsHtml: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) helps the Healthcare and Public Health (HPH) critical infrastructure sector prepare for and respond to cyber threats, adapt to the evolving threat landscape, and build a more resilient sector. As outlined in the HHS Healthcare Sector Cybersecurity concept paper, HHS is publishing these voluntary healthcare specific Cybersecurity Performance Goals (CPGs) to help healthcare organizations prioritize implementation of high-impact cybersecurity practices.

These CPGs are a voluntary subset of cybersecurity practices that healthcare organizations, and healthcare delivery organizations in particular, can prioritize to strengthen cyber preparedness, improve cyber resiliency, and ultimately protect patient health information and safety. They were built off the chassis of CISA’s CPGs and informed by common industry cybersecurity frameworks, guidelines, best practices, and strategies (e.g., Healthcare Industry Cybersecurity Practices, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework, and Healthcare and Public Health Sector Cybersecurity Framework Implementation Guide). The HPH CPGs directly address common attack vectors against U.S. domestic hospitals as identified in the 2023 Hospital Cyber Resiliency Landscape Analysis.

essentialGoals.name: Mitigate Known Vulnerabilities
essentialGoals.descr: Reduce the likelihood of threat actors exploiting known vulnerabilities to breach organizational networks that are directly accessible from the Internet.
cisa: No Exploitable Services on the Internet (2.W)

essentialGoals.name: Email Security
essentialGoals.descr: Reduce risk from common email-based threats, such as email spoofing, phishing, and fraud.

addResx.title: CISA Insights: Enhance Email & Web Security

essentialGoals.name: Multifactor Authentication
essentialGoals.descr: Add a critical, additional layer of security, where safe and technically capable, to protect assets and accounts directly accessible from the Internet.

essentialGoals.name: Basic Cybersecurity Training
essentialGoals.descr: Ensure organizational users learn and perform more secure behaviors.

essentialGoals.name: Strong Encryption
essentialGoals.descr: Deploy encryption to maintain confidentiality of sensitive data and integrity of Information Technology (IT) and Operational Technology (OT) traffic in motion.

essentialGoals.name: Revoke Credentials for Departing Workforce Members, Including Employees, Contractors, Affiliates, and Volunteers
essentialGoals.descr: Prevent unauthorized access to organizational accounts or resources by former workforce members, including employees, contractors, affiliates, and volunteers by removing access promptly.

essentialGoals.name: Basic Incident Planning and Preparedness
essentialGoals.descr: Ensure safe and effective organizational responses to, restoration of, and recovery from significant cybersecurity incidents.

essentialGoals.name: Unique Credentials
essentialGoals.descr: Use unique credentials inside organizations’ networks to detect anomalous activity and prevent attackers from moving laterally across the organization, particularly between IT and OT networks.

essentialGoals.name: Separate User and Privileged Accounts
essentialGoals.descr: Establish secondary accounts to prevent threat actor’s from accessing privileged or administrative accounts when common user accounts are compromised.

essentialGoals.name: Vendor/Supplier Cybersecurity Requirements
essentialGoals.descr: Identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with third party products and services.

enhancedGoals.name: Asset Inventory
enhancedGoals.descr: Identify known, unknown (shadow), and unmanaged assets to more rapidly detect and respond to new vulnerabilities.

enhancedGoals.name: Third Party Vulnerability Disclosure
enhancedGoals.descr: Establish processes to promptly discover and respond to known threats and vulnerabilities in assets provided by vendors and service providers.

enhancedGoals.name: Third Party Incident Reporting
enhancedGoals.descr: Establish processes to promptly discover and respond to known security incidents or breaches across vendors and service providers.
practices: Security Operations Center and Incident Response
enhancedGoals.nist: SA-4, SA-9, SR-2, SR-3, SR-5, PM-9, RA-3, SA-15, SR-2, SR-3, SR-5, SR-6

enhancedGoals.name: Cybersecurity Testing
enhancedGoals.descr: Establish processes to promptly discover and responsibly share vulnerabilities in assets discovered through penetration testing and attack simulations
practices: Security Operations Center and Incident Response

enhancedGoals.name: Cybersecurity Mitigation
enhancedGoals.descr: Establish processes internally to act quickly on prioritized vulnerabilities discovered through penetration testing and attack simulations.
practices: Security Operations Center and Incident Response
cisa: Third Party Validation of Cybersecurity Control Effectiveness (1.F)

enhancedGoals.name: Detect and Respond to Relevant Threats and Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures
enhancedGoals.descr: Ensure organizational awareness of and ability to detect relevant threats and TTPs at endpoints. Ensure organizations are able to secure entry and exit points to its network with endpoint protection.

enhancedGoals.name: Network Segmentation
enhancedGoals.descr: Mission critical assets are separated into discrete network segments to minimize lateral movement by threat actors after initial compromise.

enhancedGoals.name: Centralized Log Collection
enhancedGoals.descr: Collection of necessary telemetry from security log data sources within an organization’s network that maximizes visibility, cost effectiveness, and faster response to incidents.
practices: Security Operations Center and Incident Response

enhancedGoals.name: Centralized Incident Planning and Preparedness
enhancedGoals.descr: Ensure organizations consistently maintain, drill, and update cybersecurity incident response plans for relevant threat scenarios.
practices: Security Operations Center and Incident Response

enhancedGoals.name: Configuration Management
enhancedGoals.descr: Define secure device and system settings in a consistent manner and maintain them according to established baselines.
enhancedGoals.nist: CM-1, CM-2, CM-3, CM-4, CM-5, CM-6, CM-7, CM-9, SA-10
